description: Failing to handle SIGINT/SIGTERM so a Ctrl-C leaves a half-written output file or orphaned child processes.
body: Failure mode: the tool writes its output file in place and spawns children, but installs no signal handlers, so interrupting it leaves a truncated/corrupt file and child processes still running. Detection signals: after Ctrl-C an output file is partially written and looks valid; orphaned processes linger in the process table; a pipeline consumes a truncated file. Do not write in place without interrupt handling. Safer fallback: write to a temp file and rename atomically on success, trap SIGINT/SIGTERM to clean up temp files and terminate children, and exit with 128+signal.
